rows.snippet: 7030: The SQL Database Engine has encountered an unexpected error and is unable to continue. Check error log for more information. 22 years ago 7030: The SQL Database Engine has encountered an unexpected error and is unable to continue. Check error log for more information. March 12, 2004 | KB: 90190 https://support.laser... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/90190/7030-the-sql-database-engine ...
rows.title: 7030: The SQL Database Engine has encountered an unexpected error and is unable to continue. Check error log for more information.
rows.detailTitle: 7030: The SQL Database Engine has encountered an unexpected error and is unable to continue. Check error log for more information.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in 7030: The SQL Database Engine has encountered an unexpected error and is unable to continue. Check error log for more information. Product: Laserfiche 6, Plus 6 KB: 90190 Published: March 12, 2004 Last Updated: March 12, 2004 Workaround 1 Try the following: Contact a LaserFiche administrator. Check the error log for the subcode. Restart the LaserFiche Server. Workaround 2 If this issue occurs when trying to create a database, then you should make sure that the account assigned to the LaserFiche Server has administrative privileges to Microsoft SQL Server. You may modify the current account being used by the LaserFiche Server by performing the following steps: Contact the LaserFiche administrator. From the computer on which the LaserFiche Server was installed, open Services. Services can be found under Administrative Tools in the Control Panel. Select the LaserFiche Server service (LaserFiche for Microsoft SQL Server). From the Action menu, select Properties. Click the Log On tab. This tab allows the configuration of the Windows account assigned to the LaserFiche Server. Configure the LaserFiche Server to use a Windows account that has sufficient privileges to the local computer and SQL Server. Click OK. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

March 12, 2004 | KB: 100141 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/100141/sql-informational-and-error-messages-are-not-logge... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/100141/sql-informational-and-er ...
rows.title: SQL Informational and Error Messages are Not Logged.
rows.detailTitle: SQL Informational and Error Messages are Not Logged.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith SQL Informational and Error Messages are Not Logged. Product: Laserfiche 6, Plus 6 KB: 100141 Published: March 12, 2004 Last Updated: March 12, 2004 Summary Informational and error messages concerning SQL Server are not stored in the Event Viewer. Workaround Install LaserFiche Client, Desktop, Executive, Intuition, Notebook, or Plus version 6.03 or higher. Additionally, install LaserFiche Server version 6.03 or higher. More Information Upgrading LaserFiche will allow network databases to log informational and error messages concerning the SQL Server in the Event Viewer. However, standalone databases will not log informational or error messages concerning MSDE. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: An "Error:3169 The backed up database has on-disk structure version 611." Error Occurs When Restoring Your SQL Database Backup.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error:3169 The backed up database has on-disk structure version 611." Error Occurs When Restoring Your SQL Database Backup.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error:3169 The backed up database has on-disk structure version 611." Error Occurs When Restoring Your SQL Database Backup. Product: Laserfiche Server - Team (MSDE) 7.2, Laserfiche Server - United (MS SQL) 7.1.2, Laserfiche Server - United (MS SQL) 7.2 KB: 1011232 Published: July 3, 2006 Last Updated: July 3, 2006 Summary When restoring your SQL database backup, you may receive the following error message: Error:3169 The backed up database has on-disk structure version 611. The server supports version 539 and cannot restore or upgrade this database. Restore filelist is terminating abnormally. Cause You are trying to restore a Microsoft SQL Server 2005 backup on a Microsoft SQL Server 2000 server. This is not a supported operation. Microsoft SQL Server 2005 does not support downgrades or restores to Microsoft SQL Server 2000. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
